name: Achray
blurb: On Grand Lake on the remote east side of the park, near the Barron Canyon. Rustic sites and cabins.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

campgrounds.sub: East side · Remote · Grand Lake · Cabins

name: Arrowhead
blurb: Three campgrounds near Huntsville, with Stubb's Falls, a swimmable lake, and a famous winter ice-skating trail.
fishing: Arrowhead and Mayflower Lakes: bass, pike, rainbow trout

campgrounds.sub: North and South, Mixed, Vault toilets, Radio-free South
Electric, Cabins, Closest to Stubb's Falls
Electric, Large private sites, Near the beach

trails.name: Stubb's Falls Trail
trails.detail: Flat forest walk to the Little East River waterfall

trails.name: Big Bend Lookout
trails.detail: Short walk to a viewing deck over the Big East River gooseneck

trails.name: Mayflower Lake Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalks and climbs around a deep spring-fed lake

trails.name: Homesteaders Trail
trails.detail: Rugged terrain past abandoned 1870s farm fields

trails.name: Beaver Meadow Trail
trails.detail: Porcupine Bluffs, a beaver pond, and the old Oke homestead

name: Awenda
blurb: A forested peninsula on Georgian Bay with five beaches and six campgrounds shaded by sugar maples. Kettle's Lake is motor-free for paddling, and 30 km of trails wind to the shore.
fishing: Georgian Bay: bass, pike, walleye, panfish; Kettle's Lake: bass, panfish

campgrounds.sub: Radio-free and dog-free, Near the beaches

trails.name: Beaver Pond Trail
trails.detail: Barrier-free boardwalk loop through a beaver-shaped nature reserve

trails.name: Nipissing Trail
trails.detail: A 155-step staircase up the ancient Nipissing Bluff

trails.name: Robitaille Homestead Trail
trails.detail: Past an abandoned 1902 farmstead to 11,500-year-old dunes

trails.name: Beach Trail
trails.detail: Along the Georgian Bay shoreline with Giant's Tomb Island views

trails.name: Wendat Trail
trails.detail: Loops Kettle's Lake with a marsh boardwalk and lots of birds

trails.name: Bluff Trail
trails.detail: Long forested loop along the bluff linking the campgrounds and trails

name: Balsam Lake
blurb: A big family park on Balsam Lake, the high point of the Trent-Severn Waterway, with a long sandy beach and eight campgrounds. Good fishing for bass, walleye, and muskie.
fishing: Balsam Lake: largemouth and smallmouth bass, walleye, muskie

campgrounds.sub: Non-electric, Closest to the boat launch
Electric, A few lakeside sunrise sites

trails.name: Lookout Trail
trails.detail: Through glacial eskers and kames to a panoramic lookout

trails.name: Lorie's Link Trail
trails.detail: Fields of wildflowers and a pine plantation

trails.name: Woodland Trail
trails.detail: A forest trek with both natural and pioneer highlights

trails.name: Pond Loop Trail
trails.detail: Circles a pond full of aquatic life

name: Bon Echo
blurb: Home to the 100 m Mazinaw Rock and its Indigenous pictographs, reached by the Mugwump ferry. Two lakeside campgrounds plus walk-in and backcountry sites.
fishing: Mazinaw Lake: lake trout, bass, walleye

trails.name: Cliff Top Trail
trails.detail: Ferry or paddle to Mazinaw Rock, then stairs to three lookout decks over the pictographs

trails.name: High Pines Trail
trails.detail: Loop through pine and hemlock forest and wetlands with a Mazinaw Lake glimpse

trails.name: Shield Trail
trails.detail: Canadian Shield loop on the old Addington Road through hardwoods and a beaver swamp

trails.name: Bon Echo Creek Trail
trails.detail: Short, leisurely walk along Bon Echo Creek (can be muddy)

trails.name: Abes and Essens Lake Trail
trails.detail: Backcountry loops (Clutes, Essens, Abes) with five hike-in campsites

name: Bonnechere
blurb: An Ottawa Valley classic where the Little Bonnechere River meets Round Lake. Sandy beach, river paddling, and the Footprints in Time walks.
fishing: Round Lake: lake trout, bass, walleye

campgrounds.sub: Old white pines · 43 Electrical · Sites 37 and 39 barrier-free

trails.name: McNaughton Trail
trails.detail: Footprints in Time interpretive walk along the Little Bonnechere River

name: Bronte Creek
blurb: The most urban park in the system, in Oakville. 144 all-electric sites in four small loops, plus a Play Barn, historic farm, and trails. Day-use and campground have separate entrances; no beach.
fishing: Bronte Creek, smallmouth bass, panfish

trails.name: Gateway Trail
trails.detail: The campground trail, an easy loop close to your site

trails.name: Maiden's Blush Trail
trails.detail: Paved forest loop, wheelchair accessible (day-use area)

trails.name: Trillium Trail
trails.detail: Spring wildflowers and valley views (day-use area)

trails.name: Half Moon Valley Trail
trails.detail: Ravine loop down to Bronte Creek with a boardwalk and some hills

trails.name: Ravine Trail
trails.detail: Valley-top path to a scenic ravine lookout

name: Canisbay
blurb: KM 23. A big hardwood campground on Canisbay Lake, dog-free and radio-free, with no motorboats allowed.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

campgrounds.sub: KM 23 · Hardwood · Dog and radio-free · No motorboats

trails.name: Peck Lake
trails.detail: Circles the shoreline of Peck Lake (km 19.2)

trails.name: Track and Tower
trails.detail: Lookout over Cache Lake and old railway history (km 25)

name: Charleston Lake
blurb: A clear Canadian Shield lake in eastern Ontario near Brockville, with three wooded campgrounds, boat-in interior sites, and a strong trail network. Sandstone Island and the 10 km Tallow Rock Bay loop are highlights.
fishing: Charleston Lake: smallmouth and largemouth bass, lake trout, pike

campgrounds.sub: Younger forest, Sunnier sites, Some electric
By the beach, Electric and non-electric, Barrier-free sites

trails.name: Quiddity Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalk over a wetland to a scenic Charleston Lake lookout

trails.name: Sandstone Island Trail
trails.detail: Shoreline loop to a rock shelter used over 1,200 years ago

trails.name: Hemlock Ridge Trail
trails.detail: Hemlock ridge, a beaver pond, and sandstone rock crevices

trails.name: Shoreline Centennial Trail
trails.detail: An easy interpretive walk along the lakeshore

trails.name: Tallow Rock Bay Trail
trails.detail: The park's longest loop across the Frontenac Arch to Slim Bay's floating bridge

trails.name: Blue Mountain Trail
trails.detail: A water-access climb to the highest peak in Leeds County

name: Chutes
blurb: Waterfalls and the Seven Sisters rapids on the Aux Sables River, the only park campground on Highway 17 between Sudbury and the Sault.
fishing: Aux Sables River: pike, walleye, smallmouth bass

campgrounds.sub: Seven Sisters, Red Pine, Log Drive, And Big Chute loops · 79 Electrical · Radio-free near the falls

trails.name: Twin Bridges Trail
trails.detail: Falls, the Seven Sisters rapids, and river lookouts; short out-and-back option to the bridges

name: Darlington
blurb: A lakefront park on Lake Ontario. Three campgrounds, Lakeside (all electric), Cliffside, and Hilltop, with beaches, birding, and marsh trails. A rail line runs past the park.
fishing: Lake Ontario & McLaughlin Bay, salmon, trout, bass, pike

campgrounds.sub: All electric · Radio-free · By the beach

trails.name: Burk Trail
trails.detail: Fields, forest, and a pioneer cemetery to a Lake Ontario lookout

trails.name: Robinson Creek Trail
trails.detail: Forested ravine loop from the Visitor Centre

trails.name: Waterfront Trail
trails.detail: A section of the 350 km Lake Ontario Waterfront Trail

name: Emily
blurb: A Kawarthas family park on the Pigeon River. Warm-water swimming, easy paddling, and big grassy sites.
fishing: Pigeon River: walleye, muskie, bass, panfish

campgrounds.sub: Mixed electric · Near the beach and amphitheatre

trails.name: Marsh Boardwalk Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalk into the Pigeon River cattail marsh, great for birds and turtles

name: Fairbank
blurb: A crystal-clear, spring-fed lake on the rim of the Sudbury Basin meteorite crater. Waterfront sites you can launch a canoe from.
fishing: Fairbank Lake: smallmouth bass, lake trout

trails.name: Wa-Shai-Ga-Mog Trail
trails.detail: Bedrock climb to views of Fairbank Lake and the Fairbank Fault

name: Grundy Lake
blurb: Nine campgrounds across four inland lakes. Smooth-rock swimming, quiet water, no motorboats.
fishing: Northern pike, bass, panfish

trails.name: Swan Lake
trails.detail: Nature reserve; rocky ridges, lookouts, and a wetland boardwalk

trails.name: Gut Lake
trails.detail: Most popular; Canadian Shield rock, lakes and streams, a rope swing

trails.name: Beaver Dams
trails.detail: Forest and wetlands; a Great Blue Heron rookery and a beaver dam

trails.name: Pakeshkag Lake
trails.detail: Multi-use linear trail past Beaver Lake wetland and backcountry sites

name: Kearney Lake
blurb: KM 36.5. Mid-size and non-electric, with two beaches on Kearney Lake.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

name: Killarney
blurb: A wilderness park of pink granite and white La Cloche quartzite ridges that inspired the Group of Seven. One car campground on George Lake, plus a legendary canoe interior and the 78 km La Cloche Silhouette Trail.
fishing: George Lake and Georgian Bay: smallmouth bass, pike

campgrounds.sub: Named loops (Bear Alley, Trailer Court, ...), Yurts and cabins

trails.detail: Climbs to two lookouts over the La Cloche Mountains and Georgian Bay

trails.name: The Crack

trails.name: La Cloche Silhouette Trail
trails.detail: The legendary multi-day backcountry loop from George Lake

name: Killbear
blurb: Seven campgrounds on a Georgian Bay peninsula, windswept pines, big sand beaches. No backcountry.
fishing: Smallmouth bass, lake trout, walleye, pike, perch

campgrounds.sub: Radio-free, The largest campground, Boat launch

name: Lake Superior
blurb: A vast wilderness park on Superior's east shore. The Agawa Rock pictographs, Old Woman Bay, and some of the best day hiking in Ontario.
fishing: Lake trout, brook trout, salmon; brook trout in the inland lakes

blurb: KM 31. The most scenic corridor campground, next to the Visitor Centre on Lake of Two Rivers.

name: Long Point
blurb: A Lake Erie sandspit and UNESCO biosphere near Port Rowan, famous for birding and 2 km of soft sand beach. Four campgrounds, some right in the dunes. Beach-focused, with little hiking.
fishing: Long Point Bay and Lake Erie: bass, walleye, perch, pike

name: MacGregor Point
blurb: A Lake Huron park near Port Elgin known for the Huron Fringe Birding Festival, with three campgrounds, coastal wetlands, and sunset beaches. Trails and boardwalks wind through fen and forest.
fishing: Lake Huron: bass, pike, salmon, trout

blurb: KM 30.6. Open year-round with yurts, electrical sites, and the Old Railway Bike Trail.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

blurb: A quiet Almaguin park on Eagle Lake with three sandy beaches, island paddling, disc golf, and a great dog beach.

blurb: A Lake Ontario peninsula famous for spring and fall bird migration and monarch butterflies. Eight campgrounds, a long sandy beach, a marsh boardwalk, and a historic lighthouse.
fishing: Presqu'ile Bay and Lake Ontario: bass, pike, walleye, panfish

fishing: Restoule and Stormy Lakes: walleye, lake trout, pike, muskie, smallmouth bass

blurb: KM 40, down Rock Lake Road. Pictographs, canoe access, and a busy beach.

blurb: The world's largest baymouth sandbar, with dune beaches on Lake Ontario and calm swimming on West Lake. Books out fast.
